pi_items.source: Wikipedia, Railway Technology, FHWA project profiles, and Norfolk Southern official sources confirm the route name
Multiple authoritative sources (Wikipedia, Railway Technology, FHWA) confirm this is a railroad/rail corridor

Wikipedia states: 'The railroad focused on its former Norfolk & Western main line through western Virginia and West Virginia by improving tunnel clearances for double-stack intermodal service.' Railway Technology confirms the entire corridor was designed for double-stack intermodal operations. Route became operational for double-stack service on September 9, 2010.

Norfolk Southern official website states: 'Norfolk Southern safely ships chemicals and wastes, including petroleum products, plastics, industrial chemicals, chlorine, sulfur and related chemicals.' CAS 7782-50-5 verified as the correct CAS number for elemental chlorine (Cl2) via ChemicalBook, CAS Common Chemistry, Fisher Scientific, and EPA IRIS databases.

pi_items.metadata_value: East-West through the central business district
pi_items.verified_value: East-West corridor (Norfolk, VA to Chicago, IL), but primarily runs through Appalachian regions and mid-size cities rather than major central business districts
pi_items.source: Multiple sources document the route from Port of Norfolk through Lynchburg, Roanoke, Bluefield, Welch, Williamson, Prichard, Kenova, Ironton, Portsmouth, Rickenbacker (Columbus), Marion, Bellevue, Fort Wayne to Chicago

pi_items.metadata_value: Vinyl Chloride (mentioned in task description but not in metadata)
pi_items.verified_value: Vinyl Chloride CAS 75-01-4 is a real chemical used to manufacture PVC. Norfolk Southern's chemical transport capabilities are documented, but specific confirmation of Vinyl Chloride on Heartland Corridor not found.
pi_items.source: PubChem, CDC NIOSH, EPA OEHHA confirm Vinyl Chloride is CAS 75-01-4. No specific documentation of Vinyl Chloride transport on Heartland Corridor found in search results.
ChemicalBook, CAS Common Chemistry, Fisher Scientific, EPA IRIS, ECHA, and NOAA CAMEO Chemicals all confirm 7782-50-5 is the correct CAS number for elemental chlorine (Cl2)

privileged_info_summary: The Norfolk Southern Heartland Corridor is a real 852-km double-stack intermodal rail route connecting the Port of Norfolk, Virginia to Chicago, Illinois (with extension to Columbus, Ohio). Opened for double-stack service on September 9, 2010. Norfolk Southern confirmed transports chlorine via rail. Chlorine CAS number 7782-50-5 is correct. The route runs East-West through Appalachian regions and mid-size cities including Lynchburg, Roanoke, Bluefield, Columbus, and Fort Wayne, rather than major central business districts. The task is synthetic—it does not download or operate on real route data but tests user ability to enter data into CAMEO Data Manager GUI.
